content: Ohm's Law explains the relation between voltage, current, and resistance. It tells us that current increases when voltage increases and decreases when resistance increases.
Kirchhoff's Laws help us study complex circuits. One law tracks current at a junction, and the other tracks voltage around a closed loop.
Current electricity is the flow of electric charge through a conductor. It is commonly measured in amperes and powers many electrical devices.
Voltage is the electrical pressure that pushes charges through a circuit. A higher voltage can drive more current if resistance stays the same.
Resistance is the property that opposes the flow of current in a circuit. Materials with high resistance allow less current to pass through.
